inline_comments.message: I suggest using ID=7, as current max is 6 -- for 'VNF Scale Out'.

This method of adding entries to the database is deprecated.
Please move this statement at end of file "db.changelog-01.sql"

VID is using *liquibase* to control ongoing changes on live DB. This allows incremental DB versioning.

Result should be something like:

--changeset vid:add_workflow_pnf_software_upgrade
INSERT INTO `vid_workflow` (`WORKFLOW_DB_ID`, `WORKFLOW_APP_NAME`) VALUES (7, 'PNF Software Upgrade');


Note that the "on duplicate key" is not necessary, as liquibase controls applying the change once.
I'm pretty sure this block is not passed-through, so case is not needed to be handled here.

I suggest extracting this block to function instead of duplicating it.

The method signature, in that case, will be something like  `nativeWorkflowRequestData(modelType, requestParameters)`

It may conditionally add vnfInstanceId or pnfInstanceId field-name by modelType, OR: let the caller append it's non-common fields on the result. (I prefer the former.)

So, it is assumed that the VNFs list includes also PNF?
Is that assumption temporal (until a further VID/AAI change), or this should be valid from now on?
this change can be reverted back to "us16807000" once you have your simulator and tests setup with the given user.
is it intentional the instance id is repeated in both "pnfInstanceId" and in userParams(name="pnfId")?
I can assume this implementation is a stub, until a list is of PNFs is available? (I'm echoing my same question from above)
Done
Done
Done
Done
Have added a new method with arguments (ModelType, workflowType and vnf) . Passing ModelType as undefined as of now, until we have the PNF changes implemented. Please review if the changes are okay.
Done
It is intentional to differentiate the api endpoint parameter and the naming convention inside SO
Yes that is right
Can be removed
Done

this changeset is ok.

still, it should better be placed at the end of the file.
anyhow, in this position it breaks the previous changeset to two.

you may find these docs insightful: https://www.liquibase.org/documentation/sql_format.html

It wasn't my intention; sorry for the confusion. See my comment on line 264 below, and then re-read https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/vid/+/102674/6/vid-app-common/src/main/webapp/app/vid/scripts/modals/new-change-management/new-change-management.controller.js#291

It is ok to have here a function, but name "nativeWorkflowRequestData" is not describing the method, and params are not "on".

relatedInstanceList is not needed for pnf. Testcase fails when it is added for pnf. Since it has been added only for'vnf' ModelType. 
vnf.name is added as part of userParam in pnf case.
i'm pretty sure this block is redundant and can be removed.
